html:
Runestone, granite, 2.05 meters height, 0.95 meters width at the base (east-west), tapering upwards and with an inward curve on the northwest side, and adjacent to a runestone mound 0.55 meters thick. The inscription is oriented towards the northeast and southwest.

Location: Sweden, Södermanland County, Nyköping Municipality, Ludgo.

Rundata identification number: Sö 137.

How certain are we about this?

This is the National Heritage Board's archaeological registration of the stone itself – a physical, documented historical monument. The runic inscription itself is a contemporary primary source, but the interpretation of the text and its context may be uncertain, and the dating is based on the type and style of the runes rather than specific years.
tldr: This is a description of an ancient runestone located in Ludgo, which is protected as a historical monument. The text explains the size of the stone and its location.
lattlast:
The text describes a rune stone made of granite, located in Ludgo, Södermanland. The stone is over two meters tall and has been painted or carved on two sides. It is considered an ancient monument, meaning it is legally protected to preserve our history.

Because the stone is old, the exact year it was erected is unknown, but we can identify the style and type of runes used. The National Heritage Board has registered the stone so that information about its appearance and location will be preserved for the future.

It's important to distinguish between the physical stone itself and the interpretation of what the ancient runes actually mean. Even though we can read the symbols on the stone, it can be difficult to know exactly what the people who wrote them intended a thousand years ago.
